segments.text: Lesson 46.

translation.text: 第46课。

segments.text: Hobbies.

translation.text: 业余爱好。

segments.text: First listen, and then answer the following question.

translation.text: 先听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: Who, according to the author, are fortune's favored children?

translation.text: 作者认为谁是命运宠爱的孩子？

segments.text: A gifted American psychologist has said, Worry is a spasm of the emotion.

translation.text: 一位才华横溢的美国心理学家说过：“忧虑是情绪的痉挛。”

segments.text: The mind catches hold of something and will not let it go.

translation.text: 心灵抓住某件事不肯放手。

segments.text: It is useless to argue with the mind in this condition.

translation.text: 在这种状态下同心灵争辩毫无用处。

segments.text: The stronger the will, the more futile the task.

translation.text: 意志越强，努力越徒劳。

segments.text: One can only gently insinuate something else into its convulsive grasp.

translation.text: 只能温和地把别的东西悄悄塞进它紧抓不放的手中。

segments.text: And if this something else is rightly chosen, if it is really attended by the illumination of another field of interest, gradually, and often quite swiftly, the old undo grip relaxes and the process of

translation.text: 如果选择得当，如果它确实带来另一兴趣领域的光明，旧有而有害的紧握便会逐渐、而且常常很快地松开，

segments.text: recuperation and repair begins.

translation.text: 恢复和修复的过程随之开始。

segments.text: The cultivation of a hobby and new forms of interest is therefore a policy of the first importance to a public man.

translation.text: 因此，对公众人物而言，培养爱好和新的兴趣形式是极其重要的方针。

segments.text: But this is not a business that can be undertaken in a day, or swiftly improvised by a mere command of the will.

translation.text: 但这不是一天就能办成，也不能仅凭意志命令而仓促拼凑。

segments.text: The growth of alternative mental interests is a long process.

translation.text: 培养替代性的精神兴趣是一个漫长过程。

segments.text: The seeds must be carefully chosen.

translation.text: 种子必须精心挑选。

segments.text: They must fall on good ground.

translation.text: 必须落在肥沃土壤中。

segments.text: They must be sedulously tended if the vivifying fruits are to be at hand when needed.

translation.text: 若想在需要时收获赋予生机的果实，就必须勤勉照料。

segments.text: To be really happy and really safe, one ought to have at least two or three hobbies, and they must all be real.

translation.text: 要获得真正的幸福和安全，一个人至少应有两三种爱好，而且必须都是真正的爱好。

segments.text: It is no use starting late in life to say, I will take an interest in this or that.

translation.text: 到晚年才说“我要对这个或那个产生兴趣”毫无用处。

segments.text: Such an attempt only aggravates the strain of mental effort.

translation.text: 这种尝试只会加重精神努力的负担。

segments.text: A man may acquire great knowledge of topics unconnected with his daily work, and yet get hardly any benefit or relief.

translation.text: 一个人也许掌握大量与日常工作无关的知识，却几乎得不到任何益处或放松。

segments.text: It is no use doing what you like.

translation.text: 只做自己喜欢的事没有用。

segments.text: You have got to like what you do.

translation.text: 你必须喜欢自己所做的事。

segments.text: Broadly speaking, human beings may be divided into three classes.

translation.text: 大体说来，人类可分为三类。

segments.text: Those who are toiled to death, those who are worried to death, and those who are bored to death.

translation.text: 累死的人、愁死的人和无聊死的人。

segments.text: It is no use offering the manual laborer, tired out with a hard week's sweat and effort, the chance of playing a game of football or baseball on Saturday afternoon.

translation.text: 对辛苦流汗劳作一周、疲惫不堪的体力劳动者，提供星期六下午踢足球或打棒球的机会毫无意义。

segments.text: It is no use inviting the politician or the professional or businessman who has been working or worrying about serious things for six days to work or worry about trifling things at the weekend.

translation.text: 让连续六天为严肃事务工作或忧虑的政治家、专业人士或商人在周末为琐事工作或忧虑，也毫无用处。

segments.text: As for the unfortunate people who can command everything they want, who can gratify every caprice and lay their hands on almost every object of desire, for them a new pleasure, a new excitement is only an

translation.text: 至于那些不幸而能得到想要的一切、满足每个任性愿望、几乎能占有所有欲求之物的人，一种新快乐、新刺激只不过是

segments.text: additional satiation.

translation.text: 更多的餍足。

segments.text: In vain, they rush frantically round from place to place, trying to escape from avenging boredom by mere clatter in motion.

translation.text: 他们徒劳地疯狂奔波，试图凭运动的喧嚣逃离前来报复的无聊。

segments.text: For them, discipline in one form or another is the most hopeful path.

translation.text: 对他们而言，某种形式的纪律是最有希望的道路。

segments.text: It may also be said that rational, industrious, useful human beings are divided into two classes.

translation.text: 也可以说，理性、勤劳而有用的人分为两类。

segments.text: First, those whose work is work and whose pleasure is pleasure, and secondly, those whose work and pleasure are one.

translation.text: 第一类人的工作是工作、娱乐是娱乐；第二类人的工作与娱乐合而为一。

segments.text: Of these the former are the majority.

translation.text: 前者占多数。

segments.text: They have their compensations.

translation.text: 他们也得到补偿。

segments.text: The long hours in the office or the factory bring with them, as their reward, not only the means of sustenance, but a keen appetite for pleasure, even in its simplest and most modest forms.

translation.text: 办公室或工厂里的漫长工时带来的回报，不仅是谋生手段，还有对娱乐的强烈胃口，哪怕是最简单朴素的娱乐。

segments.text: But fortunes favored children belong to the second class.

translation.text: 但命运宠爱的孩子属于第二类。

segments.text: Their life is a natural harmony.

translation.text: 他们的生活自然和谐。

segments.text: For them, the working hours are never long enough.

translation.text: 对他们而言，工作时间永远不够长。

segments.text: Each day is a holiday, and ordinary holidays, when they come, are grudged as enforced interruptions in an absorbing vocation.

translation.text: 每天都是假日；普通假日来临时，反而被嫌弃为对迷人事业的强制打断。

segments.text: Yet to both classes, the need of an alternative outlook, of a change of atmosphere, of a diversion of effort, is essential.

translation.text: 然而对两类人来说，转换视角、改变气氛、转移精力都不可或缺。

segments.text: Indeed, it may well be that those whose work is their pleasure are those who most need the means of banishing it at intervals from their minds.

translation.text: 实际上，工作就是乐趣的人很可能最需要一种手段，定期把工作从脑海中驱走。
